note: Shared glossary for all parts (fitts) of Beowulf. Entries keyed by word form as printed in Klaeber 1922, lowercased, editorial brackets/parentheses stripped. Lemma — meaning (grammatical info).

glossary.hwæt: hwæt — what! lo! (interjection calling for attention; also the pronoun 'what')
glossary.gār-dena: Gār-Dene — of the Spear-Danes (genitive plural; gār 'spear' + Dene 'Danes')
glossary.gēardagum: gēardagas — in days of yore (dative plural; gēar 'year' + dagas 'days')
glossary.þēodcyninga: þēodcyning — of the people-kings (genitive plural; þēod 'people, nation' + cyning 'king')
glossary.gefrūnon: gefrignan — (we) have heard of, learned by inquiry (1st plural preterite)
glossary.ðā: sē/þā — the, those (nominative plural of the demonstrative); as adverb: then
glossary.ellen: ellen — courage, valor, mighty deeds (accusative singular)
glossary.scyld: Scyld — Scyld, mythical founder of the Danish royal house (nominative)
glossary.meodosetla: meodosetl — of mead-seats, mead-benches (genitive plural, object of oftēah; medu 'mead' + setl 'seat')
glossary.oftēah: oftēon — took away, deprived of (3rd singular preterite; takes a genitive object)
glossary.eorlas: eorl — warriors, nobles (accusative plural; the MS reads 'eorl' — the ending is Klaeber's emendation, hence the brackets)
glossary.syððan: siþþan — after, since, from the time when (conjunction)
glossary.wearð: weorðan — became, was (3rd singular preterite; auxiliary of the passive: 'was found')
glossary.þæs: þæt — for that, of that (genitive singular of the demonstrative: 'comfort for that')
glossary.frōfre: frōfor — comfort, consolation, help (feminine; genitive/dative/accusative singular)
glossary.gebād: gebīdan — experienced, lived to see (3rd singular preterite; takes the genitive)
glossary.wolcnum: wolcen — under the clouds, the skies (dative plural; cf. archaic English 'welkin')
glossary.weorðmyndum: weorðmynd — in honors, marks of esteem (dative plural)
glossary.him: hē — to him, or to them (dative singular or plural of hē)
glossary.ymbsittendra: ymbsittend — of the around-sitters, neighboring peoples (genitive plural; present participle of ymbsittan 'to sit around')
glossary.hronrāde: hronrād — whale-road = the sea (accusative singular; the poem's first kenning: hron 'whale' + rād 'road, riding')
glossary.hȳran: hȳran — to obey, hearken to (infinitive; takes the dative)
glossary.scolde: sculan — had to, was obliged to (3rd singular preterite)
glossary.gomban: gombe — tribute (accusative singular; a rare, old word)
glossary.ðǣm: sē — to that one, to him (dative singular of the demonstrative)
glossary.geong: geong — young (adjective; in line 20 restored — the MS is burned, only '…uma' survives)
glossary.geardum: geard — in the courts, dwellings (dative plural; geard 'yard, enclosure' — cf. middangeard 'middle-earth')
glossary.þone: sē — whom, that (accusative singular masculine of the demonstrative, here relative)
glossary.folce: folc — to the folk, people (dative singular)
glossary.fyrenðearfe: fyrenðearf — dire distress (accusative singular; fyren 'crime, torment' + ðearf 'need')
glossary.ongeat: ongietan — perceived, understood, had seen (3rd singular preterite)
glossary.þē: þe — which, that (relative particle; Klaeber prints þē, emended — the MS has only 'þ')
glossary.drugon: drēogan — suffered, endured (3rd plural preterite; cf. Scots 'dree')
glossary.aldorlēase: aldorlēas — lord-less, without a chief (aldor 'lord, elder' + -lēas '-less'; printed aldor(lē)ase — the letters in parentheses are restored from the burned MS)
glossary.lange: lang — long (adjective/adverb; lange hwīle 'a long while')
glossary.hwīle: hwīl — while, space of time (accusative singular)
glossary.līffrēâ: līffrēa — Lord of life, God (nominative singular; līf 'life' + frēa 'lord'; the circumflex is Klaeber's mark for a contracted vowel)
glossary.wealdend: wealdend — Wielder, Ruler (nominative singular; a title of God)
glossary.bēowulf: Bēowulf — Beowulf; here Scyld's son, the Danish king — not the poem's hero (nominative)
glossary.scedelandum: Scedeland — in the Scede-lands, southern Scandinavia (dative plural; cf. Skåne, Scania)
glossary.sceal: sculan — shall, must, ought by nature to (3rd singular present)
glossary.guma: guma — man (nominative singular; poetic word; printed (geong g)uma — restored from the burned MS)
glossary.gōde: gōd — good (here the noun: 'with good, by good action', dative singular)
glossary.gewyrcean: gewyrcan — to bring about, achieve, earn (infinitive)
glossary.fromum: from — splendid, bold, generous (dative plural adjective)
glossary.feohgiftum: feohgift — with treasure-gifts (dative plural; feoh 'wealth, cattle' — cognate with 'fee' — + gift)
glossary.bearme: bearm — in the bosom, lap (dative singular; in line 21 printed (bea)rme — restored from the burned MS)
glossary.ylde: yldo — old age (dative singular; on ylde 'in old age')
glossary.gewunigen: gewunian — may remain with, stand by (3rd plural present subjunctive)
glossary.wilgesīþas: wilgesīþ — willing companions (nominative plural; willa 'goodwill, desire' + gesīþ 'companion')
glossary.cume: cuman — may come (3rd singular present subjunctive)
glossary.gelǣsten: gelǣstan — may stand by, serve, follow (3rd plural present subjunctive; cf. 'last')
glossary.lofdǣdum: lofdǣd — by praise-deeds, praiseworthy deeds (dative plural; lof 'praise' + dǣd 'deed')
glossary.gehwǣre: gehwā — in each (dative singular feminine; mǣgþa gehwǣre 'in each of tribes')
glossary.geþēôn: geþēon — to thrive, prosper (infinitive; the circumflex is Klaeber's mark for a contracted vowel)
glossary.gewāt: gewītan — departed, went (3rd singular preterite; him gewāt 'took himself off')
glossary.gescæphwīle: gescæphwīl — the shaped hour, appointed time of death (dative singular; gesceap 'destiny, what is shaped' + hwīl 'time')
glossary.felahrōr: felahrōr — very vigorous, still strong (adjective; fela 'much' + hrōr 'active, strong')
glossary.frēan: frēa — of the Lord (genitive singular of frēa 'lord'; here God)
glossary.hyne: hē — him (accusative singular, variant of hine)
glossary.ætbǣron: ætberan — carried, bore (to a place) (3rd plural preterite)
glossary.brimes: brim — of the sea, the surf (genitive singular)
glossary.faroðe: faroð — to the current, the shore-water (dative singular)
glossary.þenden: þenden — while, as long as (conjunction; archaic)
glossary.wēold: wealdan — wielded, ruled (3rd singular preterite; takes the dative)
glossary.wine: wine — friend, friend-and-lord (nominative singular; wine Scyldinga 'friend of the Scyldings')
glossary.scyldinga: Scyldingas — of the Scyldings, the descendants of Scyld = the Danes (genitive plural)
glossary.landfruma: landfruma — land-chief, ruler of the land (nominative singular; land + fruma 'first one, chief')
glossary.āhte: āgan — possessed, ruled (3rd singular preterite; cognate with 'ought', 'owe')
glossary.hȳðe: hȳð — at the harbor, landing-place (dative singular; survives in place-names: Hythe, Rotherhithe)
glossary.hringedstefna: hringedstefna — the ring-prowed ship (nominative singular; hringed 'ringed, ring-adorned' + stefna 'prow')
glossary.īsig: īsig — icy (adjective; a famous crux — 'shining', 'ready', and other meanings have been proposed; no one knows)
glossary.ond: ond — and (the usual spelling of and in this MS)
glossary.ūtfūs: ūtfūs — eager to set out (adjective; ūt 'out' + fūs 'eager, ready')
glossary.æþelinges: æþeling — of the prince, atheling (genitive singular)
glossary.fær: fær — vessel, craft (nominative singular; related to faran 'to fare, travel')
glossary.þā: þā — then (adverb); also: those, the (plural demonstrative); who, which (relative)
glossary.þēoden: þēoden — lord, chief of a people (accusative singular; from þēod 'people')
glossary.bēaga: bēag — of rings (genitive plural; gold arm-rings, the currency of reward)
glossary.bryttan: brytta — dispenser, distributor (accusative singular; bēaga brytta 'giver of rings' = generous lord)
glossary.bearm: bearm — bosom, lap; hold (accusative singular; on bearm scipes 'into the ship's bosom')
glossary.mādma: māðum — of treasures (genitive plural; mādma fela 'many of treasures')
glossary.fela: fela — many, much (indeclinable; takes the genitive)
glossary.feorwegum: feorweg — from far-ways, distant regions (dative plural; feor 'far' + weg 'way')
glossary.hȳrde: hȳran — heard (1st singular preterite; ne hȳrde ic 'I have not heard')
glossary.cȳmlīcor: cȳmlīce — more becomingly, more handsomely (comparative adverb)
glossary.gegyrwan: gegyrwan — to equip, adorn, make ready (infinitive)
glossary.hildewǣpnum: hildewǣpen — with battle-weapons (dative plural; hild 'battle' + wǣpen 'weapon')
glossary.heaðowǣdum: heaðowǣd — with war-garments, armor (dative plural; heaðu 'war' + wǣd 'garment' — cf. 'widow's weeds')
glossary.billum: bill — with swords (dative plural; an old word for sword)
glossary.mid: mid — with (preposition; here after its object: him mid 'with him')
glossary.scoldon: sculan — had to, were destined to (3rd plural preterite)
glossary.flōdes: flōd — of the flood, the sea (genitive singular)
glossary.ǣht: ǣht — possession, keeping (accusative singular; from āgan 'to own')
glossary.nalæs: nalæs — not at all, by no means (adverb; from ne + ealles)
glossary.lǣssan: lǣssa — lesser, smaller (dative plural; comparative of lȳtel 'little')
glossary.tēodan: tēon — furnished, provided, adorned (3rd plural preterite)
glossary.þēodgestrēonum: þēodgestrēon — with people-treasures, treasures of a nation (dative plural; þēod 'people' + gestrēon 'treasure')
glossary.þon: þon — than (the manuscript's own form, kept by Klaeber; most editors emend to þon[ne])
glossary.frumsceafte: frumsceaft — first creation, origin, beginning (dative singular; fruma 'first' + sceaft 'shaping')
glossary.umborwesende: umborwesende — being an infant (accusative singular; umbor 'infant' + wesende, present participle of wesan 'to be')
glossary.gȳt: gȳt — yet, still, further (adverb; þā gȳt 'then still, moreover')
glossary.āsetton: āsettan — set up, placed (3rd plural preterite)
glossary.segen: segn — banner, standard (accusative singular; from Latin signum)
glossary.gyldenne: gylden — golden (accusative singular masculine; printed g(yl)denne — restored from the burned MS)
glossary.holm: holm — the sea, the deep (accusative singular)
glossary.gārsecg: gārsecg — the ocean, the open sea (accusative singular; an obscure old word, literally perhaps 'spear-man')
glossary.mōd: mōd — mind, heart, spirit (nominative singular; cf. 'mood')
glossary.cunnon: cunnan — know how to, are able (3rd plural present; cf. 'can')
glossary.sōðe: sōð — truth (dative singular; tō sōðe 'in sooth, for a truth')
glossary.selerǣdende: selerǣdend — hall-counselors (nominative plural; sele 'hall' + rǣdend 'counselor'; emended — the MS reads selerædenne)
glossary.hlæste: hlæst — load, cargo (dative singular; cf. German Last)
glossary.onfēng: onfōn — received (3rd singular preterite; takes the dative)
glossary.burgum: burg — in the strongholds, fortified dwellings (dative plural; cf. ‑borough, ‑bury in place-names)
glossary.lēodcyning: lēodcyning — people-king, king of a nation (nominative singular; lēod 'people' + cyning)
glossary.longe: lange — long, for a long time (adverb; variant spelling)
glossary.þrāge: þrāg — time, span of time (accusative singular; longe þrāge 'for a long while')
glossary.folcum: folc — among the folks, peoples (dative plural)
glossary.gefrǣge: gefrǣge — famed, known by report (adjective; from gefrignan 'to learn by asking')
glossary.ellor: ellor — elsewhither, to another place (adverb; poetic — 'fæder ellor hwearf' is a veiled way to say he died)
glossary.hwearf: hweorfan — turned, passed, went (3rd singular preterite)
glossary.aldor: aldor — lord, chief (nominative singular; the same word also means 'life' — see aldorlēase in the prologue)
glossary.earde: eard — from the land, native soil (dative singular)
glossary.oþ: oþ — until (with þæt: oþ þæt 'until'; variant spelling of oð)
glossary.onwōc: onwæcnan — awoke; was born (3rd singular preterite; with dative: 'to him was born')
glossary.healfdene: Healfdene — Healfdene, king of the Danes, father of Hrothgar (nominative; literally 'Half-Dane')
glossary.hēold: healdan — held, ruled; kept (to a place) (3rd singular preterite)
glossary.scyldingas: Scyldingas — the Scyldings, the Danish royal house and people (nominative/accusative plural)
glossary.bearn: bearn — child, son (at line 59 nominative plural 'children'; at line 67 it is instead the preterite of beirnan 'to run': 'him on mōd bearn' — 'it ran into his mind')
glossary.forðgerīmed: forðgerīmed — counted forth, in full tally (past participle; forð + gerīman 'to count' — cf. 'rime, rhyme')
glossary.worold: worold — world (accusative singular; in worold wōcun 'awoke into the world')
glossary.wōcun: wæcnan — awoke, were born (3rd plural preterite)
glossary.rǣswan: rǣswa — leader, chief (dative singular; the manuscript reads 'ræswa' — the ending is editorial, hence Klaeber's brackets)
glossary.heorogār: Heorogār — Heorogar, eldest son of Healfdene (nominative; 'army-spear')
glossary.hrōðgār: Hrōðgār — Hrothgar, king of the Danes, builder of Heorot (nominative)
glossary.hālga: Hālga — Halga, third son of Healfdene (nominative; 'the holy one' — the Helgi of Norse legend)
glossary.onelan: Onela — of Onela, a king of the Swedes (genitive; printed On]elan — the first letters are editorial restoration)
glossary.cwēn: cwēn — queen, noble wife (nominative singular; the ancestor of 'queen')
glossary.heaðo-scilfingas: Heaðo-Scilfing — of the War-Scylfing (genitive singular, archaic -as ending; the Scylfings are the Swedish royal house)
glossary.healsgebedda: healsgebedda — neck-bedfellow, beloved consort (nominative singular; heals 'neck' + gebedda 'bed-companion')
glossary.herespēd: herespēd — war-luck, success in arms (nominative singular; here 'army' + spēd 'success' — the old sense of 'speed', as in 'Godspeed')
glossary.weorðmynd: weorðmynd — honor, mark of esteem (nominative singular; the prologue had the dative plural weorðmyndum)
glossary.winemāgas: winemǣg — friend-kinsmen, dear kinsmen (nominative plural; wine 'friend' + mǣg 'kinsman')
glossary.hȳrdon: hȳran — obeyed (3rd plural preterite; takes the dative)
glossary.oðð: oð — until (a doubled spelling of oð that Klaeber prints here; oðð þæt 'until')
glossary.sēo: sēo — the, that (nominative singular feminine of the demonstrative)
glossary.geogoð: geogoð — youth; the young warriors as a body (nominative singular; the ancestor of 'youth')
glossary.magodriht: magodriht — band of young retainers (nominative singular; magu 'young man' + driht 'troop')
glossary.healreced: healreced — hall-building (accusative singular; heall 'hall' + reced 'building' — a poetic word)
glossary.hātan: hātan — to command, order; also to call, name (infinitive)
glossary.wolde: willan — would, wished to (3rd singular preterite)
glossary.medoærn: medoærn — mead-house (accusative singular; medu 'mead' + ærn 'house, building')
glossary.yldo: ylde — of men (genitive plural; yldo bearn 'the children of men'; a poetic word related to eald 'old')
glossary.innan: innan — within, inside (adverb; þǣr on innan 'therein')
glossary.eall: eall — all, everything (here accusative singular neuter)
glossary.gedǣlan: gedǣlan — to deal out, share, distribute (infinitive)
glossary.swylc: swylc — such (as) (pronoun: 'such as God gave him')
glossary.sealde: sellan — gave, handed over (3rd singular preterite; the old sense of 'sell')
glossary.būton: būton — except, but for (preposition; from be + ūtan 'outside')
glossary.folcscare: folcscaru — folk-share, the people's common land (accusative singular; folc + scearu 'share' — not the king's to give)
glossary.feorum: feorh — the lives (dative plural of feorh 'life')
glossary.gefrægn: gefrignan — (I) heard tell, learned by inquiry (1st singular preterite; the prologue's gefrūnon is its plural)
glossary.gebannan: gebannan — to command, summon by proclamation (infinitive; cf. 'ban', marriage 'banns')
glossary.manigre: manig — of many a (dative singular feminine)
glossary.middangeard: middangeard — middle-earth, the world of men (accusative singular; midd 'middle' + geard 'enclosure' — the human world set in the middle of things)
glossary.folcstede: folcstede — folk-stead, dwelling of the people (accusative singular)
glossary.fyrste: fyrst — time, appointed span (dative singular; on fyrste 'in due time')
glossary.gelomp: gelimpan — happened, came to pass (3rd singular preterite)
glossary.ealgearo: ealgearo — all-ready, fully finished (adjective; eall + gearo 'ready')
glossary.healærna: healærn — of hall-buildings (genitive plural; healærna mǣst 'greatest of halls')
glossary.scōp: scieppan — shaped, created; gave (a name) (3rd singular preterite — the same verb used of God's creating, and the same word as scop 'poet')
glossary.heort: Heort — Heorot, 'the Hart', Hrothgar's great hall (accusative; heorot 'stag')
glossary.sē: sē — he, that one; the (nominative singular masculine of the demonstrative; sē þe 'he who')
glossary.þe: þe — who, which, that (the indeclinable relative particle)
glossary.bēot: bēot — vow, boast (accusative singular; the formal public promise of the Germanic warrior)
glossary.ālēh: ālēogan — belied, left unfulfilled (3rd singular preterite; bēot ālēogan 'to break one's vow' — the great disgrace)
glossary.bēagas: bēag — rings (accusative plural; gold arm-rings, the currency of reward)
glossary.dǣlde: dǣlan — dealt out, distributed (3rd singular preterite; cf. 'deal')
glossary.symle: symbel — at the feast, banquet (dative singular)
glossary.hlīfade: hlīfian — towered, rose high (3rd singular preterite)
glossary.horngēap: horngēap — horn-spacious, wide-gabled (adjective; the gable-ends perhaps crowned with horns or antlers)
glossary.heaðowylma: heaðowylm — of battle-surges, hostile flame (genitive plural; heaðu 'war' + wylm 'surging, welling' — here the surge of fire)
glossary.bād: bīdan — awaited, abode (3rd singular preterite; takes the genitive: the hall 'awaited' the fire)
glossary.lāðan: lāð — of the hateful, loathed (genitive singular; cf. 'loath')
glossary.lenge: lenge — near, at hand (adjective; 'ne wæs hit lenge þā gēn' — 'it was not yet at hand')
glossary.gēn: gēn — yet, still (adverb; þā gēn 'as yet')
glossary.se: se — the, that (nominative singular masculine; unaccented spelling of sē)
glossary.ecghete: ecghete — edge-hate, sword-hatred (nominative singular; ecg 'edge, blade' + hete 'hate'; the manuscript reads 'secg' — the reading is emended)
glossary.āþumswēoran: āþumswēoran — son-in-law and father-in-law (a pair-compound: āþum 'son-in-law' + swēor 'father-in-law'; partly editorial — the manuscript's 'aþum swerian' does not construe)
glossary.wælnīðe: wælnīð — slaughter-malice, deadly feud (dative singular; wæl 'the slain' + nīð 'enmity')
glossary.ellengǣst: ellengǣst — bold spirit, powerful demon (nominative singular; ellen 'courage, strength' + gǣst 'spirit'; found only here — possibly a slip for ellorgǣst 'spirit from elsewhere')
glossary.earfoðlīce: earfoðlīce — with hardship, grievously (adverb; earfoð 'hardship' — cognate with German Arbeit 'work')
glossary.geþolode: geþolian — endured, suffered (3rd singular preterite; cf. archaic 'thole')
glossary.dōgora: dōgor — of days (genitive plural; dōgora gehwām 'on each of days')
glossary.gehwām: gehwā — each, every one (dative singular; takes a partitive genitive)
glossary.drēam: drēam — joy, mirth, festive music (accusative singular; the ancestor of 'dream', but meaning communal joy)
glossary.scopes: scop — of the scop, the court poet-singer (genitive singular)
glossary.cūþe: cunnan — knew, knew how to (3rd singular preterite)
glossary.frumsceaft: frumsceaft — first creation, origin (accusative singular; fruma 'first' + sceaft 'shaping' — the prologue used its dative)
glossary.fīra: fīras — of men, of living men (genitive plural; poetic word)
glossary.feorran: feorran — from afar, from far back (adverb); at line 156 it is instead the verb feorran 'to remove, put far away' (infinitive)
glossary.cwæð: cweðan — said, declared (3rd singular preterite; cf. archaic 'quoth')
glossary.ælmihtiga: Ælmihtig — the Almighty (nominative singular, weak form)
glossary.worhte: wyrcan — wrought, made (3rd singular preterite; printed worh(te) — the last letters are restored at the manuscript's burned edge)
glossary.wlitebeorhtne: wlitebeorht — beauty-bright, radiantly fair (accusative singular masculine; wlite 'beauty' + beorht 'bright')
glossary.wang: wang — plain, open field (accusative singular; poetic word)
glossary.bebūgeð: bebūgan — encircles, bends around (3rd singular present; the sea ringing the world's plain)
glossary.lēoman: lēoma — light, gleam, lamp (accusative; sun and moon as the world's lamps)
glossary.landbūendum: landbūend — for the land-dwellers (dative plural; land + būend 'dweller', from būan 'to dwell')
glossary.foldan: folde — of the earth, ground (genitive singular; poetic word)
glossary.scēatas: scēat — corners, regions, expanses (accusative plural; literally 'sheets, laps')
glossary.cynna: cynn — of kinds, races (genitive plural; cynna gehwylcum 'for each of kinds')
glossary.gehwylcum: gehwylc — for each (dative singular; with partitive genitive)
glossary.þāra: sē — of those (genitive plural of the demonstrative)
glossary.ðe: þe — who, which, that (the relative particle, here spelled ðe)
glossary.cwice: cwic — alive, living (nominative plural; 'quick' as in 'the quick and the dead')
glossary.hwyrfaþ: hwyrfan — move about, go, turn (3rd plural present)
glossary.drihtguman: drihtguma — retainers, men of the troop (nominative plural; driht 'troop' + guma 'man')
glossary.ðæt: þæt — that (conjunction, here spelled ðæt; oð ðæt 'until')
glossary.ān: ān — one, a certain one (nominative singular; the ancestor of 'one')
glossary.fyrene: fyren — crime, outrage, wickedness (accusative; fyrene fremman 'to work crimes')
glossary.fēond: fēond — fiend, enemy (nominative singular; literally 'the hating one' — present participle of fēogan 'to hate')
glossary.helle: hell — hell (dative/genitive singular; fēond on helle 'fiend in hell')
glossary.grimma: grimm — grim, fierce (nominative singular masculine, weak form)
glossary.gǣst: gǣst — spirit, demon (nominative singular; easily shading into gæst 'guest, stranger' — a pun the poet may intend)
glossary.grendel: Grendel — Grendel (nominative; the name's meaning is unknown — perhaps related to grindan 'to grind')
glossary.hāten: hātan — called, named (past participle; wæs hāten 'was called')
glossary.mearcstapa: mearcstapa — mark-stepper, walker of the borderlands (nominative singular; mearc 'boundary, march' + stapa 'stepper')
glossary.fīfelcynnes: fīfelcynn — of the monster-race (genitive singular; fīfel 'monster' + cynn 'kin')
glossary.wonsǣlī: wonsǣlig — unblessed, luckless (nominative singular; won- 'lacking' + sǣlig 'blessed' — the word that became 'silly')
glossary.wer: wer — man (nominative singular; as in 'werewolf', man-wolf — Grendel called simply 'a man')
glossary.weardode: weardian — occupied, kept, dwelt in (3rd singular preterite; cf. 'ward, warden')
glossary.siþðan: siþþan — after, ever since (conjunction; variant spelling)
glossary.scyppend: Scyppend — the Shaper, the Creator (nominative singular; from scieppan 'to shape')
glossary.forscrifen: forscrīfan — proscribed, condemned (past participle; literally 'written against' — scrīfan is a Latin loan, cf. 'shrive')
glossary.cāines: Cāin — of Cain (genitive; the manuscript reads 'caines' altered from 'cames' — Cain and Ham confused by the scribes)
glossary.cynne: cynn — kin, race (dative singular; in Cāines cynne 'among Cain's kin')
glossary.cwealm: cwealm — killing, violent death (accusative singular; related to 'quell')
glossary.drihten: Drihten — the Lord (nominative singular; from driht 'troop' — the leader of the heavenly host)
glossary.slōg: slēan — slew, struck (3rd singular preterite; cf. 'slay')
glossary.gefeah: gefēon — rejoiced, had joy (3rd singular preterite; takes the genitive: 'got no joy of that feud')
glossary.þǣre: sēo — of/for that (genitive or dative singular feminine of the demonstrative)
glossary.fǣhðe: fǣhð — feud, blood-feud (singular; the legal state of hostility that follows a killing — related to 'foe')
glossary.forwræc: forwrecan — drove away, banished (3rd singular preterite)
glossary.metod: Metod — the Measurer, God (nominative singular; fate or God as the one who metes out)
glossary.þȳ: sē/þæt — that (instrumental singular; for þȳ māne 'for that crime')
glossary.fram: fram — from, away from (preposition, here following its object: mancynne fram 'from mankind')
glossary.untȳdras: untȳdre — mis-broods, evil progeny (nominative plural; un- + tūdor 'offspring')
glossary.onwōcon: onwæcnan — awoke, sprang forth (3rd plural preterite)
glossary.eotenas: eoten — ettins, ogres (nominative plural; giant man-eating creatures — 'ettin' survives in dialect and fantasy)
glossary.ylfe: ylf — elves (nominative plural; here sinister night-spirits, not fair folk)
glossary.orcnēâs: orcnēas — corpse-monsters, restless dead (nominative plural; orc 'demon' — where Tolkien found his orcs — + nē(o) 'corpse'; the circumflex is Klaeber's contraction mark)
glossary.gīgantas: gīgant — giants (nominative plural; a Latin loan-word — the gigantes of Genesis, destroyed in the Flood)
glossary.wið: wið — against; with (preposition; wið Gode 'against God')
glossary.wunnon: winnan — strove, fought (3rd plural preterite; 'win' in its old sense, 'to struggle')
glossary.ðæs: þæt — for that (genitive singular of the demonstrative, spelled ðæs)
glossary.lēan: lēan — reward, requital (accusative singular; him ðæs lēan forgeald 'paid them the reward for that' — grim understatement)
glossary.forgeald: forgieldan — repaid, requited (3rd singular preterite; cf. 'yield')
glossary.nēosian: nēosian — to seek out, visit (infinitive; takes the genitive)
glossary.syþðan: siþþan — after, when (conjunction; another of its spellings)
glossary.niht: niht — night (singular; also dative plural nihtum at line 167)
glossary.becōm: becuman — came, arrived (3rd singular preterite; the old sense of 'become')
glossary.hēân: hēah — of the high (genitive singular weak, contracted form; the circumflex is Klaeber's mark for the contraction)
glossary.hring-dene: Hring-Dene — the Ring-Danes (nominative plural; the Danes as wearers of ring-mail or receivers of rings — another alliterative name for the same people)
glossary.bēorþege: bēorþegu — beer-taking, the drinking of beer (dative singular; bēor + þegu 'partaking')
glossary.gebūn: gebūan — settled into, occupied (past participle; related to būan 'to dwell')
glossary.æþelinga: æþeling — of the princes, athelings (genitive plural)
glossary.gedriht: gedriht — company, troop of retainers (accusative singular)
glossary.swefan: swefan — to sleep (infinitive; poetic word — related to Latin sopor)
glossary.symble: symbel — feast, banquet (dative singular; variant spelling of symle)
glossary.cūðon: cunnan — knew (3rd plural preterite; sorge ne cūðon 'they knew not sorrow')
glossary.wonsceaft: wonsceaft — misery, wretched lot (accusative singular; won- 'lacking' + gesceaft 'what is shaped, one's lot')
glossary.wiht: wiht — creature, being, wight (nominative singular; at line 186 the same word means 'a whit, at all': wihte gewendan 'change a whit')
glossary.unhǣlo: unhǣlu — of un-health, evil, perdition (genitive singular; wiht unhǣlo 'creature of evil' — hǣlu is 'health, salvation')
glossary.sōna: sōna — at once, immediately (adverb; the old sense of 'soon')
glossary.rēoc: rēoc — savage, furious (adjective; a rare word)
glossary.ræste: ræst — rest, resting-place, bed (dative singular at line 122, accusative at 139)
glossary.genam: geniman — took, seized (3rd singular preterite; cf. archaic 'nim')
glossary.hrēmig: hrēmig — exultant, gloating (adjective; takes the dative: hūðe hrēmig 'exulting in loot')
glossary.hām: hām — home (tō hām 'homeward'; the ancestor of 'home')
glossary.wælfylle: wælfyll — fill of slaughter (dative singular; wæl 'the slain' + fyll 'fill, feast')
glossary.wīca: wīc — of dwellings, lairs (genitive plural; cf. ‑wick, ‑wich in place-names)
glossary.nēosan: nēosan — to seek out (infinitive; variant of nēosian; takes the genitive)
glossary.ūhtan: ūhta — the hour before dawn (dative singular; on ūhtan 'in the dark before daybreak' — a time important enough to own a word)
glossary.ǣrdæge: ǣrdæg — early day, daybreak (dative singular; mid ǣrdæge 'with the first of day')
glossary.gūðcræft: gūðcræft — war-craft, violent strength (nominative singular; gūð 'war' + cræft)
glossary.undyrne: undyrne — unhidden, plainly known (adjective; un- + dyrne 'secret')
glossary.wiste: wist — feasting, abundance (dative singular; æfter wiste 'after the feast' — from wesan 'to be': well-being)
glossary.wōp: wōp — weeping, lamentation (nominative singular; related to 'weep')
glossary.āhafen: āhebban — raised, lifted up (past participle; cf. 'heave'; wōp ūp āhafen 'weeping was raised up')
glossary.morgenswēg: morgenswēg — morning-sound, morning-cry (nominative singular; morgen 'morning' + swēg 'sound')
glossary.ǣrgōd: ǣrgōd — good from of old, preeminently good (adjective; ǣr 'before, of old' + gōd)
glossary.þolode: þolian — suffered, endured (3rd singular preterite; cf. archaic 'thole'; the ge- form appears at line 87)
glossary.ðrȳðswȳð: ðrȳðswȳð — strength-mighty, very strong (adjective; ðrȳð 'might' + swȳð 'strong' — of Hrothgar: strong, and still helpless)
glossary.þegnsorge: þegnsorg — thane-sorrow, grief for one's retainers (accusative singular; a king's particular grief)
glossary.drēah: drēogan — endured, suffered (3rd singular preterite; cf. Scots 'dree' — the prologue's drugon is its plural)
glossary.syðþan: siþþan — after, when (conjunction; yet another spelling)
glossary.lāst: lāst — track, footprint (accusative singular; the shoemaker's 'last' is the same word)
glossary.scēawedon: scēawian — beheld, examined (3rd plural preterite; the ancestor of 'show')
glossary.wergan: werga — of the accursed (genitive singular weak; wergan gāstes 'of the accursed spirit')
glossary.gāstes: gāst — of the spirit, demon (genitive singular)
glossary.lāð: lāð — hateful, loathsome (adjective; lāð ond longsum 'loathsome and long-lasting')
glossary.næs: ne wæs — was not (contraction of ne + wæs)
glossary.fyrst: fyrst — space of time, interval, respite (nominative singular)
glossary.ymb: ymb — after, about, around (preposition; ymb āne niht 'after one night')
glossary.gefremede: gefremman — carried out, perpetrated (3rd singular preterite)
glossary.morðbeala: morðbealu — murder-bale, murderous harm (morð 'killing by stealth' — the ancestor of 'murder', the worst crime in Germanic law — + bealu 'bale, harm')
glossary.nō: nō — not at all, by no means (emphatic negative)
glossary.mearn: murnan — mourned, felt remorse (3rd singular preterite; the prologue's murnende is its participle)
glossary.fore: fore — for it, on that account (adverb)
glossary.fæst: fæst — fast, fixed, firm (adjective; wæs tō fæst on þām 'was too fixed in them')
glossary.þām: sē/þæt — them, those; that (dative of the demonstrative)
glossary.ēaðfynde: ēaðfynde — easy to find (adjective; ēað 'easy' + findan 'find')
glossary.gerūmlīcor: gerūmlīce — more roomily, at a roomier distance (comparative adverb; rūm 'room, space')
glossary.sōhte: sēcan — sought (3rd singular preterite; the word is supplied by editors — Klaeber's brackets — where the manuscript lost a verb)
glossary.būrum: būr — among the bowers, outbuildings (dative plural; a būr is a separate sleeping-chamber — the ancestor of 'bower')
glossary.gebēacnod: gebēacnian — signaled, made known (past participle; from bēacen 'beacon, sign' — cf. 'beckon')
glossary.sweotolan: sweotol — by a clear, evident (dative singular; variant of swutol)
glossary.tācne: tācen — token, sign (dative singular; sweotolan tācne 'by a clear token')
glossary.healðegnes: healðegn — of the hall-thane (genitive singular; bitterly ironic — Grendel as Heorot's most regular 'retainer')
glossary.fæstor: fæste — more securely (comparative adverb; 'fast' as in 'hold fast')
glossary.ætwand: ætwindan — escaped, wound away from (3rd singular preterite; takes the dative; cf. 'wind')
glossary.rīxode: rīxian — reigned, ruled (3rd singular preterite; from rīce 'kingdom' — the verb of kings, given to Grendel)
glossary.rihte: riht — right, justice (dative singular; wið rihte 'against right')
glossary.wan: winnan — strove, made war (3rd singular preterite; also at line 151: Grendel wan 'Grendel warred')
glossary.āna: āna — alone (adverb; āna wið eallum 'alone against all')
glossary.hūsa: hūs — of houses (genitive plural; hūsa sēlest 'best of houses' — Heorot's standing epithet)
glossary.sēlest: sēlest — best (superlative; its comparative sēlla has no positive — 'good' is understood)
glossary.hwīl: hwīl — while, span of time (nominative singular)
glossary.twelf: twelf — twelve (the manuscript writes the Roman numeral '.XII.'; Klaeber spells it out)
glossary.wintra: winter — of winters (genitive plural; years are counted in winters)
glossary.tīd: tīd — tide, span of time (accusative singular; twelf wintra tīd 'the space of twelve winters')
glossary.wēana: wēa — of woes (genitive plural; wēana gehwelcne 'each of woes')
glossary.gehwelcne: gehwylc — each (accusative singular masculine; variant spelling)
glossary.sīdra: sīd — of wide, vast (genitive plural; sīdra sorga 'of wide sorrows')
glossary.secgum: secg — to men (dative plural; a poetic word for 'man, warrior'; supplied by editors — Klaeber's brackets — where the manuscript dropped a word)
glossary.ylda: ylde — of men (genitive plural; ylda bearnum 'to the children of men')
glossary.cūð: cūð — known (adjective, old past participle of cunnan; 'uncouth' once meant simply 'unknown')
glossary.gyddum: gydd — in songs, lays (dative plural; gyddum geōmore 'mournfully in songs')
glossary.geōmore: geōmore — sadly, mournfully (adverb; the prologue's geōmor is its adjective)
glossary.þætte: þætte — that (conjunction; fused from þæt + þe)
glossary.hrōþgār: Hrōþgār — Hrothgar (accusative; spelled with þ here — Klaeber preserves the manuscript's own variation between ð and þ)
glossary.hetenīðas: hetenīð — hate-enmities, deeds of malice (accusative plural; hete 'hate' + nīð 'enmity, malice')
glossary.wæg: wegan — waged, carried on (3rd singular preterite; literally 'carried' — the old sense of 'weigh')
glossary.missēra: missēre — of half-years, seasons (genitive plural; fela missēra 'many a season')
glossary.sæce: sacu — strife, contention (accusative singular; related to 'sake' — originally a legal dispute)
glossary.sibbe: sibb — peace, kinship-peace (accusative singular; the bond among kin — cf. 'sibling')
glossary.manna: mann — of men (genitive plural; manna hwone 'any of men')
glossary.mægenes: mægen — of the might, force (genitive singular; cf. 'main' in 'might and main')
glossary.deniga: Dene — of the Danes (genitive plural, longer form; the prologue's Gār-Dena is the same people)
glossary.feorhbealo: feorhbealu — life-bale, deadly evil (accusative singular; feorh 'life' + bealu 'harm')
glossary.fēa: feoh — with fee, payment (instrumental of feoh 'wealth, cattle' — the prologue's feohgiftum carries the same word)
glossary.þingian: þingian — to settle, come to terms (infinitive; the legal verb of negotiating compensation — from þing 'assembly, court')
glossary.nǣnig: nǣnig — no, none, not any (pronoun; ne + ǣnig 'any')
glossary.witena: wita — of the counselors, wise men (genitive plural; the king's advisory council was the witan)
glossary.wēnan: wēnan — to expect, hope for (infinitive; takes the genitive; cf. archaic 'ween')
glossary.þorfte: þurfan — needed, had cause to (3rd singular preterite)
glossary.beorhtre: beorht — of bright, splendid (genitive singular feminine)
glossary.bōte: bōt — compensation, remedy (genitive singular; the legal word for making good a wrong — it survives in 'to boot')
glossary.banan: bana — of the slayer (genitive singular; the word behind 'bane'; the manuscript reads 'banū' — the ending is editorial)
glossary.folmum: folm — from the hands (dative plural; poetic word)
glossary.ǣglǣca: ǣglǣca — the awesome one, fearsome assailant (nominative singular; one of the poem's most disputed words — later applied to Beowulf himself, so 'monster' cannot be its whole meaning)
glossary.duguþe: duguð — the seasoned warriors, veterans (accusative singular, collective; from dugan 'to be worthy' — cf. 'doughty')
glossary.geogoþe: geogoð — the young warriors (accusative singular, collective; duguð ond geogoð 'old guard and young' — the whole warband, in a rhyme)
glossary.seomade: seomian — lurked, hovered, hung about (3rd singular preterite)
glossary.syrede: syrwan — plotted, ambushed (3rd singular preterite; from searu 'cunning, device')
glossary.sinnihte: sinniht — in perpetual night (dative singular; sin- 'ever-' + niht)
glossary.mistige: mistig — misty (accusative plural; the ancestor of 'misty')
glossary.helrūnan: helrūne — hell-mysteries, beings versed in hell's secrets (nominative plural; hell + rūn 'secret, whispered mystery' — the word behind 'rune')
glossary.hwyrftum: hwyrft — in their goings, turnings (dative plural; hwyrftum scrīþað 'glide on their rounds')
glossary.scrīþað: scrīþan — glide, prowl (3rd plural present; the poem's eerie verb for how monsters move — smooth, soundless)
glossary.fyrena: fyren — of crimes (genitive plural; fela fyrena 'many of crimes')
glossary.mancynnes: mancynn — of mankind (genitive singular; fēond mancynnes 'the enemy of mankind')
glossary.āngengea: āngengea — lone-goer, solitary walker (nominative singular; ān 'one, alone' + gengea 'goer')
glossary.hȳnða: hȳnðu — of humiliations, injuries (genitive plural; from hēan 'lowly, abject')
glossary.heorot: Heorot — Heorot, Hrothgar's hall (accusative; 'the Hart' — spelled Heort at its naming in line 78)
glossary.eardode: eardian — inhabited, dwelt in (3rd singular preterite; from eard 'homeland')
glossary.sincfāge: sincfāg — treasure-adorned, gold-gleaming (accusative singular; sinc 'treasure' + fāg 'decorated, stained')
glossary.sweartum: sweart — in the swart, black (dative plural; cf. 'swarthy')
glossary.nihtum: niht — nights (dative plural; sweartum nihtum 'in the black nights')
glossary.gifstōl: gifstōl — gift-seat, throne (accusative singular; gifu 'gift' + stōl 'seat, stool' — the throne from which treasure is dealt)
glossary.mōste: mōtan — might, was permitted to (3rd singular preterite; the ancestor of 'must')
glossary.māþðum: māðum — treasure, precious thing (accusative singular; the prologue's mādma is its genitive plural)
glossary.metode: Metod — the Measurer, God (dative singular; 'for Metode' — 'because of God', if that is what it means; the line is a famous crux)
glossary.myne: myne — mind, desire, favor (accusative singular; related to gemynd 'memory' — whose myne, and of what, is the crux of a famously dark line)
glossary.wisse: witan — knew (3rd singular preterite, variant form of wiste)
glossary.wrǣc: wrǣc — misery, persecution (nominative singular; related to wrecan 'to drive out' — the ancestor of 'wretch' and kin to 'wreak')
glossary.mōdes: mōd — of the heart, spirit (genitive singular)
glossary.brecða: brecð — breaking (mōdes brecða 'breaking of heart' — as close to 'heartbreak' as Old English comes)
glossary.fremman: fremman — to do, perpetrate, work (infinitive; fyrene fre(m)man 'to work crimes' — the m in parentheses is restored where the manuscript is damaged)
glossary.monig: manig — many a one (nominative singular; variant spelling monig)
glossary.rīce: rīce — mighty one, powerful man (nominative singular; the same word means 'kingdom, power' — cf. bishopric, German Reich)
glossary.rūne: rūn — at counsel, secret deliberation (dative singular; rīce tō rūne 'the mighty at counsel'; the word behind 'rune')
glossary.rǣd: rǣd — counsel, plan, 'rede' (accusative singular; Æthelred 'the Unready' was un-rǣd — 'ill-counseled')
glossary.swīðferhðum: swīðferhð — for the strong-souled, brave (dative plural; swīð 'strong' + ferhð 'spirit, soul')
glossary.fǣrgryrum: fǣrgryre — against the sudden terrors (dative plural; fǣr 'sudden attack' — softened into 'fear' — + gryre 'terror')
glossary.gefremmanne: gefremman — to do, perform (inflected infinitive; tō gefremmanne 'to be done')
glossary.hwīlum: hwīlum — at times, sometimes (adverb; the dative plural of hwīl — 'at whiles')
glossary.gehēton: gehātan — vowed, promised (3rd plural preterite; the noun bēot belongs to this verb's world)
glossary.hærgtrafum: hærgtræf — at the heathen shrines (dative plural; hærg 'idol, sanctuary' + træf 'tent, building'; the manuscript's 'hrærg' is a scribal slip)
glossary.wīgweorþunga: wīgweorþung — idol-worship, honor paid to idols (accusative; wīg 'idol' (a different word from wīg 'war') + weorþung 'honoring')
glossary.bǣdon: biddan — prayed, entreated (3rd plural preterite; the prologue's bæd is its singular)
glossary.gāstbona: gāstbona — soul-slayer — the devil (nominative singular; gāst 'soul' + bona 'slayer': what the poet calls the god the Danes prayed to)
glossary.þēodþrēaum: þēodþrēa — against the people-calamity, national torment (dative; þēod 'people' + þrēa 'affliction')
glossary.hǣþenra: hǣþen — of the heathens (genitive plural; possibly from hǣð 'heath' — the people of the wild places)
glossary.hyht: hyht — hope (nominative singular; from hycgan 'to think, hope')
glossary.gemundon: gemunan — remembered, were mindful of (3rd plural preterite; related to 'mind')
glossary.mōdsefan: mōdsefa — in the mind's thought (dative singular; mōd + sefa — two words for the inner self, doubled)
glossary.cūþon: cunnan — knew (3rd plural preterite; Metod hīe ne cūþon 'the Measurer they knew not')
glossary.dēmend: Dēmend — the Judge, Deemer (nominative singular; from dēman 'to judge' — cf. 'deem, doom')
glossary.helm: helm — Helm, protector (nominative singular; literally 'helmet' — God as covering of the heavens)
glossary.waldend: wealdend — Wielder, Ruler (nominative singular; spelled waldend here — the prologue's wealdend is the same title of God)
glossary.wā: wā — woe (interjection and noun; wā bið þǣm ðe… 'woe be to him who…')
glossary.bið: bēon — is, will be (3rd singular present; the form Old English uses for general truths)
glossary.nīð: nīð — malice, affliction, violent enmity (accusative singular)
glossary.fæþm: fæþm — embrace, bosom (accusative singular; also 'fathom' — the span of outstretched arms)
glossary.wihte: wiht — a whit, at all (dative of wiht used adverbially; the same word as wiht 'creature' at line 120)
glossary.gewendan: gewendan — to change, turn (infinitive; cf. 'wend, went')
glossary.wēl: wēl — well (adverb; wēl bið 'well be it')
glossary.mōt: mōtan — may, is permitted to (3rd singular present)
glossary.dēaðdæge: dēaðdæg — death-day (dative singular; æfter dēaðdæge 'after his death-day')
glossary.fæþmum: fæþm — in the embraces, arms (dative plural; the fire's fæþm three lines earlier, now the Father's)
glossary.wilnian: wilnian — to desire, ask for (infinitive; from willa 'will, desire')
